[ID:1152048-H-4331] Referral Order 03/24/2026 To Provider From Provider AVERY HOME HEALTH MAIN OFAI 5660 W FLAMINGO RD STE A 3175 SAINT ROSE PKWY STE. 320 LAS VEGAS, NV 89103 HENDERSON, NV 89052-3500 Phone: Phone: (702) 997-9833 Phone: (702) 416-0611 Fax: (702) 666-0413 Fax: Fax: (702) 899-4479 Referral Order Information Diagnosis Postoperative wound dehiscence, subsequent encounter ICD-10: T81.31XD: Disruption of external operation (surgical) wound, not elsewhere classified, subsequent encounter Order Name Orders included: 1 Postoperative wound dehiscence, subsequent encounter ICD-10: T81.31XD: Disruption of external operation (surgical) wound, not elsewhere classified, subsequent encounter WOUND CARE REFERRAL Schedule Within: provider's discretion Note to Provider: please initiate wound vac therapy on a non-healing diabetic foot wound and perform MWF Notes please initiate wound vac therapy on a non-healing diabetic foot wound and perform MWF Patient Information Patient Name MARSHALL, JEFFREY Sex- DOB - Age M 10/21/1957 68yo Address 5025 KELL LN LAS VEGAS, NV 89115-6703 Phone H: (307) 277-6300 M: (307) 277-6300 Primary Insurance *SELF PAY* Secondary Insurance None recorded. Electronically Signed by: AUSTIN SOLOMON, DPM 03-24-2026 5:20 PM ET athena 17028994479 pg 2 of 3 ORTHOPEDIC FOOT AND ANKLE INSTITUTE 3175 SAINT ROSE PKWY, HENDERSON NV 89052-3500 MARSHALL, Jeffrey (id #30081, dob: 10/21/1957) 92 Encounters and Procedures Clinical Encounter Summaries Encounter Date: 03/24/2026 Patient Name MARSHALL, JEFFREY (68yo, M) ID# Appt. Date/Time 03/24/2026 10:15AM 30081 DOB 10/21/1957 Service Dept. MAIN OFAI Provider AUSTIN SOLOMON, DPM Insurance Med Primary: *SELF PAY* Prescription: SURESCRIPTS LLC - This member could not be found in the payer's files. Please verify coverage and all member demographic information. details Chief Complaint left foot Vitals None recorded. Allergies Reviewed Allergies Medications None recorded. Problems Reviewed Problems Postoperative wound dehiscence, initial encounter - Onset: 03/02/2026 Cellulitis of toe of left foot - Onset: 03/02/2026 Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic peripheral angiopathy with gangrene, unspecified whether long term insulin use - Onset: 03/02/2026 Family History Family History not reviewed (last reviewed 03/02/2026) Social History Social History not reviewed (last reviewed 03/02/2026) Surgical & Procedure History Surgical & Procedure History not reviewed (last reviewed 03/02/2026) Past Medical History Past Medical History not reviewed (last reviewed 03/02/2026) Screening Name Score Notes STEADI Fall Risk Not scored HPI Patient is a 68yoM who presents today following left partial 5th ray amputation due to gangrene on 2/13/26. He 03-24-2026 5:20 PM ET athena 17028994479 pg 3 of 3 ORTHOPEDIC FOOT AND ANKLE INSTITUTE 3175 SAINT ROSE PKWY, HENDERSON NV 89052-3500 MARSHALL, Jeffrey (id #30081, dob: 10/21/1957) ambulates in normal shoegear today no surgical shoe as his last one broke. No new complaints. No recent N/V/F/C/SOB/CP. ROS None recorded. Physical Exam Examination of the left lower extremity reveals lightly palpable DP/PT pulses. Gross sensation is not intact. Gross motor function is intact. Surgical site is clean and dry. No erythema noted around the distal lateral aspect of the incision. Incisions are approximated with dehiscence noted at the distal aspect which is now a full-thickness wound with a fibrogranular base. No direct probe-to-bone. No active drainage. No areas of fluctuance or crepitus. Procedure Documentation BS Wound debride: Sharp, excisional debridement was performed today in the office. Debridement was carried down to, and including, the level of subcutaneous tissue. All necrotic, fibrotic, and non-viable tissue was removed until the wound base exhibited healthy, bleeding, granular tissue. Hemostasis was achieved with pressure to the wound and a dressing was then applied consisting of wet to dry dressing. Assessment / Plan 68yoM, diabetic, presenting for a postoperative visit following left partial 5th ray amputation on 2/13/26. The surgical site was evaluated and is healing with full-thickness dehiscence on the distal aspect of the incision, without clinical sign of infection. The patient was instructed to continue WBAT in CAM boot/post op shoe. Given the depth of the wound, I will prescribe negative pressure wound therapy (wound vac), a wound care clinic performing vac changes MWF. Wound care instructions were reinforced, including keeping the area clean and dry and monitoring for any signs of infection such as redness, drainage, or increasing pain. A gradual transition plan was outlined, with anticipated local wound care until complete healing. He requested to go back to work he states he now has a sitting scooter he can use so he does not have to walk around as much. This is okay as long as he wears his CAM boot when he does have to ambulate. If he has any worsening symptoms, we will likely have to keep him off work for a longer duration. The patient was advised to follow up in 2 week for repeat evaluation. All questions were addressed, and the patient verbalized understanding of the recovery timeline and activity restrictions. 1. Cellulitis of toe of left foot L03.032: Cellulitis of left toe 2. Postoperative visit Z48.89: Encounter for other specified surgical aftercare Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic peripheral angiopathy with gangrene, unspecified 3. whether long term insulin use E11.52: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic peripheral angiopathy with gangrene 4. Postoperative wound dehiscence, subsequent encounter T81.31XD: Disruption of external operation (surgical) wound, not elsewhere classified, subsequent encounter WOUND CARE REFERRAL Schedule Within: provider's discretion Note to Provider: please initiate wound vac therapy on a non-healing diabetic foot wound and perform MWF Return to Office AUSTIN SOLOMON, DPM for FOLLOW UP at MAIN OFAI on 04/07/2026 at 10:30 AM Encounter Sign-Off Encounter signed-off by AUSTIN SOLOMON, DPM, 03/24/2026.
